mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing equity risk premium and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending capacity.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136214.0 as of June 2012 shows ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive labor momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months produce negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 1.0% signals extreme monetary accommodation that compresses equity discount rates and increases relative attractiveness of equities versus fixed income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.24% as of June 2012 indicates non-accommodation regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) signals synchronized demand acceleration across consumer and construction sectors, indicating broad-based economic strength that drives positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 369865.0 and HOUST at 1588.0 as of June 2012
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in both RSAFS and HOUST produces negative 20-day returns in > 52% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining more than 2% month-over-month signals supply-side contraction and reduced corporate capacity utilization, creating negative earnings revisions and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines > 2% produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.8% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, triggering equity risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% as of June 2012 indicates below-trigger regime
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: UNRATE > 4.8% produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate above 4.0% signals monetary tightening cycle that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward earnings multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 201.7 and FEDFUNDS at 5.24% as of June 2012
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Joint condition of high inflation and elevated rates produces negative 60-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized signals late-cycle slowdown and reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11443.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2012
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth < 2.0% produces negative 60-day returns in < 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) expanding above 1,500 thousand units combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals consumer confidence and residential investment acceleration, indicating demand-driven expansion that produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through forward earnings revisions.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1588.0 and RSAFS at 369865.0 as of June 2012
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in housing and consumer sectors
failure_condition: Joint condition of strong housing and retail sales produces positive 5-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.
